zh.lifeSummary: 當Ponce de la Marche於1091年9月1日出生在法國阿奎泰時，她的父親Roger le Poitevin37歲，母親Almodis de la Marche29歲。她於1123年與Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她於1109年住在法國諾門迪。她去世於1138年3月15日在法國努維勒-阿吉泰哈倫特安古利e享年46歲，埋葬在法國努維勒-阿吉泰哈倫特拉 庫洛阿巴易 諾特-達 德 拉 庫洛。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Marche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   March  .

variants.name: March
meanings: English: habitational name from March (Cambridgeshire) or from any of numerous minor English placenames deriving from Middle English marche ‘boundary’ (partly from Old English mearc in the dative-case form mearce, partly from Old French marche). Compare Mark 2.
Catalan: from the personal name March, Catalan equivalent of Mark 1. This surname is also found in southern France.
German: topographic name from Middle Low German march, Middle High German marc(h) ‘mark, sign, boundary’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word (Bavaria).

nameMeanings.name: Ponce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Carlos, Francisco, Jorge, Miguel, Pedro, Raul, Roberto.

Spanish and French: from the medieval personal name Ponce, ultimately from Pontius, a Roman surname, perhaps an ethnic name for someone from Pontus (named from Greek pontos ‘ocean’) in Asia Minor, or an Italic cognate of Latin Quintus ‘fifth’ (i.e. ‘fifth-born’). The name was borne by two 3rd-century Christian saints, a Carthaginian deacon and a martyr of Nice, but was not widely popular in the Middle Ages because of the inhibiting influence of the even more famous Pontius Pilate. In some cases though, the surname may have been originally used for someone who had played the part of this character in a religious play.

variants.name: Ponce de Leon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Cesar, Mercedes, Alberto, Francisco, Javier, Juan, Lazaro, Leticia, Manuel, Ramon.
Spanish (Ponce de León): compound of the surname Ponce + the habitational name León (see Leon ).

History: Juan Ponce De León, the Spanish explorer who discovered Florida, came from a noble Aragonese family. He is believed to have been on board for Columbus's second voyage to Hispaniola in 1493. After his death in Florida from a wound from an Indian arrow in 1521, Ponce De León's title and rights in Florida were passed on to his son.

variants.name: Points
meanings: English (of Norman origin):
from the Old French personal name Ponz, Ponce, from Latin Pontius (see Ponce ).

habitational name from any of several places called Ponts (such as those in Manche and in Seine-Maritime, Normandy), from Latin pontes ‘bridges’. This name has been established since the 13th century in Ireland, where it appears as Pounce and Pounche, and is Gaelicized as Púinse.

variants.name: Pons
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Pedro, Ramon, Guillermo, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Mario, Rafael, Apolonia.  French Adrien, Armand, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Marcelle, Matilde.
Catalan, French (Occitan), and Dutch: from the medieval personal name Pons (see Ponce ).
German: patronymic from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name, such as Bonhard, formed with the element bon ‘request, petition’ (compare Latin bonus).

variants.name: Ponzio
meanings: From Latin Pontius, a family name of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Poncelet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Benoit.  German Erwin, Fritz.
French (mainly northeastern) and Walloon:
topographic name from a diminutive of Old French poncel ‘little bridge’, or a habitational name from (Le) Poncelet, the name of several places in the northeastern part of France.

variants.name: Poinsett
meanings: Altered form of French Poinset: from a pet form of the personal name Poinse, from Latin Pontius (see Ponce ). Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. The surname Poinset is very rare in France.

History: The poinsettia takes its name from an American diplomat, Joel Poinsett (1779–1851), who was a keen amateur botanist and who brought the flower back from Mexico, where he was US ambassador. He was born in Charleston, SC, and was descended from a Huguenot, Pierre Poinset, who was born in Soubise in Charente-Maritime and settled in Charleston c. 1700. Pierre Poinset is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar registers of the Huguenot Society of America and of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Point
meanings: French: from the personal name Point, from Latin Pontius (see Ponce ).
English: variant of Points with loss of final -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Ponce de la Marche was born on 1 September 1091, in Aquitaine, France, her father, Roger le Poitevin, was 37 and her mother, Almodis de la Marche, was 29. She married Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME in 1123. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She lived in Normandy, France in 1109. She died on 15 March 1138, in Angoulême, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, at the age of 46, and was buried in Abbaye Notre-Dame de La Couronne, La Couronne, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ponce de la Marche est née le 1 septembre 1091, à Aquitaine, France, son père, Roger le Poitevin, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Almodis de la Marche, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME en 1123. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle a vécu à Normandie, France en 1109. Elle est décédée le 15 mars 1138, à Angoulême, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterrée à Abbaye Notre-Dame de La Couronne, La Couronne, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France.

Sie hat 1123 Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1109 hat sie in Normandie, Frankreich gelebt. Sie starb am 15. März 1138 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Angoulême, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich und wurde in Abbaye Notre-Dame de La Couronne, La Couronne, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich begraben.

Lei sposato Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME nel 1123. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Vissuto a Normandie, Francia nel 1109. Morì il 15 marzo 1138, nel Angoulême, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolta a Abbaye Notre-Dame de La Couronne, La Couronne, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia.

ko.Abbaye Notre-Dame de La Couronne, La Cou: 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 처트 라 쿠론 애브베이 노트레-다 데 라 쿠론
ko.lifeSummary: Ponce de la Marche(이)가 1091년 9월 1일 프랑스 아키테인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger le Poitevin(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Almodis de la Marche(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1123년 Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1109년도에 프랑스 노먼디에서 살았다. 그녀는 1138년 3월 15일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 처트 앤굴e에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 처트 라 쿠론 애브베이 노트레-다 데 라 쿠론에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ponce de la Marche nasceu em 1 de setembro de 1091, em Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Roger le Poitevin, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Almodis de la Marche, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME em 1123. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela viveu em Normandie, França em 1109. Ela faleceu em 15 de março de 1138, em Angoulême, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, com 46 anos, e foi sepultada em Abbaye Notre-Dame de La Couronne, La Couronne, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ponce de la Marche родилась 1 сентября 1091 г. в местности аквитеин, Франция, её отцу по имени Roger le Poitevin было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Almodis de la Marche, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME в 1123 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она жила в местности норманди, Франция в 1109 году. Она умерла 15 марта 1138 г. в местности ангоулeм, чарент, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция в возрасте 46 лет и была похоронена в местности эббеи нотре-дэм де ла коуронн, ла коуронн, чарент, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция.

Se casó con Wulgrin II d'ANGOULEME en 1123. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Vivió en Normandie, Francia en 1109. Murió el 15 de marzo de 1138, en Angoulême, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultada en Abbaye Notre-Dame de La Couronne, La Couronne, Charente, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia.
